summary: Backend proxy + Node bundle validated via external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All 9 tests pass (health, auth login/me, search, dashboard, basket/optimize). No 503 'Backend service is starting' fallback observed. Fix appears working end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py spawn-on-startup relies on port-8002 detection; if the Node bundle crashes after startup, proxy will resume 503 fallback (as designed). Consider a healthcheck-based restart loop for prod resilience.

/health is native FastAPI (returns {status:ok}) but externally only /api/* routes through ingress, so /health externally hits the frontend. This is expected but might confuse ops - use /api/health for external liveness.
test_credentials: admin@procureai.com / Admin@123 (login works, JWT returned, /api/auth/me confirms user)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Externally, only /api/* is proxied through ingress; /health at the external URL hits the frontend HTML. Use /api/health for external health checks or hit /health on http://localhost:8001 internally. Basket optimize requires top-level 'category' (not per-item). Search requires 'category' in body. Node backend confirmed running on 8002 via preview supervisor program.
rca of the issue: N/A - production 503 fix validated. All /api endpoints reachable via proxy; no fallback triggered.
